title: Untitled

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) The complete administrative human resources and personnel files, including original resumes, hiring letters, disciplinary records, and official separation/termination documents, for former Legal Director William Sieloff, as well as his successor, Colleen Rosshirt.

2) Any internal memorandums, emails, audit reports, or official correspondence sent by former Legal Director Colleen Rosshirt to then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or any sitting judge, that identify, warn of, or discuss the court’s non-compliance with local rules, Rules of Superintendence, Ohio law, or procedural errors, sent from 1/02/2024 through her final date of employment.

3) Any separation agreement, severance agreement, financial settlement, non-disclosure agreement, or documentation of paid administrative leave pertaining to the departure or termination of former Legal Director Colleen Rosshirt.

4) All official court job descriptions, organizational charts, and approved salary scales/pay bands for the "Legal Director" position as it existed during the tenure of William Sieloff, alongside any subsequent, revised job descriptions, organizational charts, or salary scales reflecting the restructuring, downgrading, or reallocation of the Legal Director's duties following the departure of Colleen Rosshirt.

5) Any internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or memorandums sent or received by then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or the HR Director on or around the date of Colleen Rosshirt’s separation from employment discussing her termination, the decision to leave the Legal Director position vacant, or the decision to reduce the pay and management functions associated with the position.

6) Any internal communications, HR directives, or payroll records demonstrating the specific date on which the "Legal Director" position's management functions were officially removed and the position's associated salary was reduced following the departure of Colleen Rosshirt.

7) All records related to the initial hiring of Kelli Johnson in 2018, including but not limited to her employment application, resume, references provided, and the results of any pre-employment criminal background checks or fingerprinting conducted by Franklin County.

8) Any internal emails or memorandums sent or received by then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins or the Court Director in 2018 discussing the creation of the bailiff position for Kelli Johnson, or the decision to hire her without a traditional competitive public posting for said position.

9) Any and all records of disciplinary action, "write-ups," or performance improvement plans in Kelli Johnson's personnel file from 2018 through her resignation in 2026, including any documented history of "unprofessional conduct," "aggression," or "hostility" prior to the March 24, 2026, altercation.

10) Any internal incident reports, HR reports, or memorandums generated by court staff regarding a physical altercation occurring on the 6th floor of the courthouse on March 24, 2026, involving Kelli Johnson.

11) The security camera and hallway camera video footage depicting the 6th-floor hallway immediately outside of Judge Monica Hawkins’ courtroom between the hours of 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM on March 24, 2026. (Note: Given that the contents of this footage have already been disclosed to local media and the Franklin County Sheriff, the requester objects to any preemptive claim of a 'security record' exemption).

12) The complete administrative human resources and personnel file for former bailiff Kelli Johnson, specifically including her official resignation letter submitted on or around March 30, 2026.

13) Any separation agreement, severance agreement, financial settlement, non-disclosure agreement, or documentation of paid administrative leave pertaining to Kelli Johnson’s departure from employment.

14) A comprehensive list of the names and job titles of all court employees, magistrates, and judicial staff who have executed (signed) the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" implemented in March 2026.

15) Any and all signed copies of the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" for every court employee. (Note: The requester explicitly consents to the redaction of personal home addresses, personal phone numbers, and Social Security Numbers to expedite this request).

16) Any and all distribution lists used to disseminate the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" to court employees in March 2026.

17) Any and all records, including emails, memos, or internal correspondence, documenting court employees who refused to sign, objected to, or failed to return the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement."

18) Any internal administrative emails, spreadsheets, or memorandums generated between March 1, 2026, and the present date tracking, documenting, or discussing court employees who have refused to sign the NDA, or communications discussing potential disciplinary consequences for those specific employees.

19) A current roster of all active court employees, magistrates, and judicial staff, including their names and job titles, as of the date of this request.

20) All internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or digital correspondence discussing the Court’s response to media inquiries from The Columbus Dispatch, local television news stations, or independent journalists regarding the March 24, 2026, bailiff altercation or the implementation of the employee NDA.

21) Any "talking points," prepared statements, or "media response drafts" created by Court Administration, the HR Director, or outside consultants to address public or press inquiries concerning then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins or the conduct of court staff since March 1, 2026.

22) Any calendar entries, meeting invites, or logs for "emergency meetings" or "administrative sessions" involving the Domestic Relations judges held between March 24, 2026, and March 30, 2026, to discuss media relations or the public disclosure of court incidents.

23) Any internal memorandums, meeting minutes, or emails sent between the sitting judges of the Domestic Relations Division between March 1, 2026, and the present date regarding the transition of the Administrative Judge role.

24) Any formal 'Notice of Resignation' or correspondence from Monica Hawkins to the Supreme Court of Ohio or the Franklin County Clerk of Courts officially relinquishing her duties as Administrative Judge.

25) Any and all records of 'Leave of Absence' or 'Sick Leave' taken by Judge Monica Hawkins between January 1, 2026, and the present.

26) Any formal human resources grievances, hostile work environment complaints, or internal complaints of unprofessional conduct filed by any court employee against Judge Monica Hawkins or the Court Director from January 1, 2023, to the present.

27) Copies of any and all 'Exit Interviews', separation surveys, or exit questionnaires completed by departing employees of the Domestic Relations Division between January 1, 2024, and the present date. (To preemptively resolve any statutory exemptions and expedite processing, the requester explicitly consents to the redaction of the departing employees' names, addresses, and Social Security Numbers).

28) Any and all electronic correspondence (including emails and attachments) sent or received by Judge Monica Hawkins, or her administrative staff, between June 1, 2025, and March 31, 2026, containing any of the following terms: 'Rule 5(F)', 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan', 'reporting plan', 'law enforcement database', or 'Sup.R. 5(F)'.

29) All communications, meeting requests, calendar entries, or memorandums sent between the office of Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins and the Franklin County Clerk of Courts, the Franklin County Sheriff's Office, or the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) regarding the drafting, planning, or implementation of the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement & Compliance Plan' mandated by Sup.R. 5(F), spanning June 1, 2025, to March 31, 2026.

30) Any preliminary drafts, outlines, or working documents created or reviewed by Judge Monica Hawkins or court administration staff intended to serve as the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan' or the associated local rule required by Sup.R. 5(F).

31) The agendas, meeting minutes, and distributed materials for any judges' meetings, committee meetings, or administrative meetings within the Franklin County Domestic Relations and Juvenile Branch where Sup.R. 5(F) or the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan' was listed as an agenda item or discussed, from June 1, 2025, to present.

32) Any directives, memorandums, or internal policies issued by Judge Monica Hawkins regarding the specific procedures and timelines for reporting Domestic Violence Civil Protection Orders (DVCPOs), Juvenile Civil Protection Orders, or mental health adjudications to law enforcement databases pursuant to the newly adopted Sup.R. 5(F).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe

title: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
tags: public records audit: officer personnel and conduct files (2024-2026)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following records for the period of January 1, 2024, through April 1, 2026, regarding all full-time sworn officers currently employed by your department:
Disciplinary Records: Any internal affairs investigations, citizen complaints, or disciplinary actions, including the nature, status, and final outcome of each.

Use of Force: All Use of Force reports (also known as Tactical Response Reports) involving these officers.

Personnel Files: The complete personnel files for these officers, including but not limited to: hire dates, training certificates, commendations, and performance evaluations.

Exemption Note: Per 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), the disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Please redact only private information (SSNs, home addresses, personal phone numbers) as required by law while releasing all records related to their professional conduct and training.

Format & Fees
I request that these records be provided in PDF format via MuckRock's electronic delivery system. As a non-commercial requester:

The first 50 pages of any scanned records are free (5 ILCS 140/6).

Search and redaction time may not be charged (5 ILCS 140/6(b)).

I look forward to your response within five (5) business days.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

title: Federal Inter-Agency MOUs, Joint Task Forces, and Grant Funding (Escambia County Sheriff's Office)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Florida Sunshine Law Request: Federal Inter-Agency MOUs, Joint Task Forces, and Grant Funding

To the Public Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following administrative public records regarding inter-agency cooperation between your department and federal law enforcement or intelligence agencies.

To ensure a precise search, this request does not seek active investigative files or exempt intelligence. It seeks only the following high-level administrative agreements and financial records generated or active between October 1, 2022, and the present date:

1. Inter-Agency MOUs and Task Force Agreements
A copy of all active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), Joint Task Force agreements, operational charters, or deputization agreements between your agency and the following federal entities:

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) (including the JTTF or Safe Streets)

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), specifically including Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) and Customs and Border Protection (CBP)

The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)

The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF)

The United States Secret Service (USSS) (including Cyber Fraud or Financial Crimes Task Forces)

The United States Marshals Service (USMS)

2. Watchlisting, Intelligence Sharing, and Database Protocols
Any localized administrative guidelines, training memorandums, or standard operating procedures (SOPs) provided to or generated by your agency regarding the utilization, nomination, or encountering protocols for federal threat databases. This includes, but is not limited to, the Terrorist Screening Database (TSDB), the El Paso Intelligence Center (EPIC), or specialized FinCEN (Financial Crimes Enforcement Network) reporting portals.

3. Federal Grant and Equipment Funding
Copies of budgetary allocations, grant award letters, or procurement logs detailing federal funds, equipment, or software provided to your agency by the Department of Justice (DOJ) or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) in support of joint task force operations (e.g., Byrne JAG grants, Operation Stonegarden, or UASI funding).

If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Because the Florida Public Records Act does not contain a statutory public interest fee waiver, I am requesting to mitigate costs through electronic production.

Please produce all responsive records in a native electronic format (e.g., PDF) and transmit them via email or this digital portal. I do not request physical paper copies, thereby eliminating duplication fees under §119.07(4)(a).

If this request will incur a "Special Service Charge" for extensive clerical or supervisory assistance under §119.07(4)(d), please provide a detailed, itemized estimate of those costs prior to processing the request.

If the estimated cost exceeds $20.00, please pause processing and contact me so that we may work together to narrow the scope of the request to eliminate unnecessary labor fees.
Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

title: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

FOIA Request: Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

To the FBI FOIA/PA Division:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I am requesting access to specific administrative, statistical, and procurement records pertaining to the operations of the Pensacola Resident Agency (RA), which operates under the jurisdiction of the Jacksonville Field Office, focusing on activities within Escambia County, Florida.

To avoid undue burden and ensure a precise search, this request does not seek raw intelligence, personally identifiable information (PII), or active investigative files. Instead, it seeks the following high-level administrative and statistical records generated between October 1, 2022, and the present date:

1. Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics
A statistical breakdown or summary report of the number of investigative cases opened, closed, or currently active at the Pensacola RA, categorized by FBI Classification Code or programmatic area. Specifically, I am requesting the raw case count numbers for operations falling under:

Counterintelligence (CI)

Counterterrorism (CT)

Cyber Crime

Violent Crime / Safe Streets

Public Corruption

2. Watchlisting and Task Force Administration

A copy of all active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), Joint Task Force agreements, or operational charters between the FBI (Jacksonville FO / Pensacola RA) and the Escambia County Sheriff’s Office (ECSO) and/or the Pensacola Police Department (PPD).

Any localized administrative guidelines, memorandums, or statistical reports regarding the management, nomination, or auditing of the localized Terrorist Screening Database (TSDB) / "threat watchlisting" protocols in conjunction with Escambia County local law enforcement partners.

3. Procurement, Inventory, and Budgetary Allocation

Copies of localized procurement logs, purchase orders, or budgetary allocations specifically designated for the Pensacola RA.

Vendor contracts or equipment leases originating from or executed for the Pensacola RA, specifically pertaining to IT administration, network infrastructure, office space leases, and surveillance/telecommunications hardware.

Fee Waiver Request:
I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government—specifically, how federal law enforcement resources, budget allocations, and inter-agency task forces are being utilized in the Escambia County region. This request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If any portion of this request is denied or heavily redacted, please provide a detailed justification citing the specific FOIA exemptions applied. If the search for any specific item is deemed too broad, please process the remaining items and contact me to refine the scope of the delayed item.

Thank you for your lawful compliance and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

title: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

An unredacted, complete copy of City of Seattle Supplier Contract #000000000003640 (and all associated change orders) between the City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise, Inc., originally dated on or about November 5, 2024.

To clarify, this request specifically seeks the full, unredacted content of the following sections which were redacted in previous disclosures:
 - Attachment C – Pricing
 - Attachment D – Evidence.com Terms of Use
 - Section 1.26 of Attachment A, Statement of Work (SOW)
 - All Service Level Agreements (SLA) or supplemental terms incorporated by reference into [that] agreement."

Please note:
 - Pricing is Public Post-Award: Under Washington law, pricing for "goods and services" purchased with public funds is not a trade secret under RCW 42.56.270 once a contract has been awarded and executed. The public has a paramount interest in knowing the financial terms of surveillance technology procurement.
 - Terms of Use are Not Exempt: Terms of Use (Attachment D) are not proprietary business methods; they are the legal rules governing the Agency’s use of public data. Under City of Sedro-Woolley v. Rodriguez (2025), data "paid for by" and "generated for the benefit of" the City is a public record regardless of storage location or vendor-imposed terms.
 - Prohibition on Contractual Confidentiality: An agency cannot "contract away" its statutory obligations under the PRA. Any clause in the Axon contract promising confidentiality for non-exempt information is void as a matter of public policy.
 - Narrow Construction of Exemptions: Per RCW 42.56.030, the PRA must be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and exemptions must be interpreted narrowly. The City bears the burden of proving that every redacted sentence—including "Terms of Use"—meets a specific statutory exemption.
 - Constructive Possession: Under the Nissen and O'Neill frameworks, the City maintains "constructive possession" of all terms and data within the Axon/Evidence.com ecosystem. Redacting the terms that define this possession (the SOW and Terms of Use) is a violation of the "fullest assistance" mandate in RCW 42.56.100.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

title: FOIA request: FBI case number 192C-DN-46497 (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Privacy Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a, I hereby request copies of all records in the custody or control of the Federal Bureau of Investigation — including records held at FBI Headquarters and at the FBI Denver Field Office — pertaining to the following matter:

Primary case: FBI case number 192C-DN-46497, an armed robbery of a Wells Fargo armored vehicle that occurred on or about July 20, 1993, in Denver, Colorado, investigated by the FBI Denver Field Office.

I request all records associated with or cross-referenced to this case number, including but not limited to:

1/ The complete investigative case file for 192C-DN-46497, including all serials, sub-files, and attachments.
2/ All FD-302 investigative interview reports generated in connection with this case, including any photographic lineup or photo array administered to any Wells Fargo employee, driver, or bystander, and the documented results of those procedures.
3/ All FD-36 teletypes, Electronic Communications (ECs), and AIRTEL communications sent to or from FBIHQ or any field office referencing this case number, including any communications between the FBI Denver Field Office and the FBI Los Angeles Field Office.
4/ All FD-430 statistical and summary reports associated with this case.
5/ All surveillance logs, SOG operational records, and target identification documents associated with this case.
6/ All suspect identification records, including photographs, composite sketches, fingerprint analysis results, and physical description summaries for all participants — including any unidentified third participant believed to have been involved in the robbery.
7/ All inter-agency communications between the FBI Denver Field Office and any other federal or local law enforcement agency referencing this case or the July 20, 1993 Wells Fargo robbery.
8/ All records reflecting any connection between this Denver investigation and any investigation conducted by the FBI Los Angeles Field Office, including all cross-index references in which case number 192C-DN-46497 appears in connection with Los Angeles Field Office case numbers 91A-LA-197438, 91A-LA-198172, 91A-LA-205209, 192C-LA-185807, and 192C-LA-195797.
9/ All evidence logs, chain of custody records, and evidence disposition records associated with this case, including any records reflecting the retention, transfer, or destruction of physical or photographic evidence.
10/ All records reflecting the current status, closure, or disposition of this investigation, including any closure communications, referral records, and any records reflecting a reopening of the case after February 28, 1997.
11/ To the extent any responsive records have been destroyed, all destruction authorization forms, evidence management reports, and records documenting the date, authorization, and method of destruction.

Subjects of interest: This request concerns an investigation into the armed robbery of a Wells Fargo armored vehicle in Denver, Colorado, on or about July 20, 1993. FBI records already in my possession from the Los Angeles Field Office indicate that this case was investigated in connection with Emil Decebal Matasareanu (DOB approximately July 19, 1966; SSN 571-45-4679; birthplace Timisoara, Romania) and Larry Eugene Phillips Jr. (DOB September 20, 1970), as well as at least one additional unidentified individual believed to have participated in the robbery. Both named individuals are deceased as of February 28, 1997. No Privacy Act exemption applies to either.

If the FBI processes this request in installments, I request that the following records be prioritized in the first release: all FD-302 interview reports, all photographic lineup records and their results, all teletypes and Electronic Communications between the Denver Field Office and the FBI Los Angeles Field Office, and all records identifying or describing participants beyond the two named subjects above.

I request that all responsive records be processed for maximum disclosure consistent with FOIA, and that any withholdings be identified with specificity. I request that Exemption 7(C) be applied only as to living, uncharged individuals, and note that the two primary named subjects are deceased.

I am willing to pay reasonable duplication fees. If fees are anticipated to exceed $100.00, please contact me before processing. I request a fee waiver on the grounds that disclosure of these records contributes significantly to public understanding of law enforcement operations and is not primarily in my commercial interest.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

title: FBI RS 4.10.26

communications.communication:
Dear FOIA Officer:

You are receiving this correspondence as a request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. subsection 552. I am requesting the following:

Copies of all Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) records, documents, and communications responsive to Colonel Raymond Starrett Sleeper, aka Raymond Starrat Sleeper, aka Raymond Sterritt Sleeper, aka Raymond S. Sleeper (September 13-1914-April 14, 1995; SSN 001-14-5228).

Col. Sleeper served as the Commanding Officer of the United States Air Force's Foreign Technology Division (FTD), the Deputy Chief of the Strategic Vulnerability Branch in its Air Intelligence Division, and faculty at the Air War College. Additionally, he maintained personal affiliations with the Institute for American Strategy, later known as the American Security Council Foundation, and the Western Goals Foundation.

Col. Sleeper wrote on the subject of Soviet psychological warfare, publishing books like "A Lexicon of Marxist-Leninist Semantics" and "Mesmerized by the Bear: The Soviet of Strategy of Deception".

Proof of death is not required as Col. Sleeper's date of birth was over 100 years ago; however, his gravestone is listed on a public database, Find a Grave, and can be found here: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/149575947/raymond-starrett-sleeper

I am asking you waive any fees associated with this request. I am a member of the news media. My bylines, which include Unicorn Riot, Covert Action Magazine, and the Geopolitical Economy Report, can be found in both print and electronic publications, and I am sending this request in coordination with Muckrock, an award-winning and nationally-recognized news organization. Please take note of the Office of Management and Budget guidelines published March 27, 1987 (52 FR 10012) that include electronic publications and other nontraditional publishers as representatives of the news media.

A fee waiver is appropriate in situations where "disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government, and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester,” 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(iii). While with the FTD, Col. Sleeper was briefly in charge of overseeing the United States Air Force's Project Blue Book, a study into unidentified flying objects (UFOs). Historical disclosures by the United States Air Force and Central Intelligence Agency indicate programs like Project Blue Book were used to deceive the American public about the existence (or non-existence) of UFOs. A 1994 report issued by the Air Force, "The Roswell Report: Case Closed", states that a 1947 UFO crash near Roswell, New Mexico, was in fact a "compartmented, sensitive effort" involving balloon reconnaissance research named Project MOGUL, according to pages 24 through 29. A copy can be found here:  https://www.dafhistory.af.mil/Portals/16/documents/AFD-101201-038.pdf

Germane to Project Blue Book, a 1997 article in the Central Intelligence Agency's "Studies in Intelligence" journal titled "CIA's Role in the Study of UFO's, 1947-1990" observes collaboration between agency officials and Blue Book investigators. Gerald Haines, the article's author, writes on page 73: "The early U-2s were silver (they were later painted black) and reflected the rays from the sun, especially at sunrise and sunset. They often appeared as fiery objects to observers below. Air Force BLUE BOOK investigators aware of the secret U-2 flights tried to explain away such sightings by linking them to natural phenomena such as ice crystals and temperature inversions. By checking with the Agency's U-2 Project Staff in Washington, BLUE BOOK investigators were able to attribute many UFO sightings to U-2 flights. They were careful, however, not to reveal the true cause of the sighting to the public." A copy of the article can be found here: https://www.cia.gov/resources/csi/static/cia-role-study-UFOs.pdf

Attached is correspondence, in the form of a letter taken from Project Blue Book's archives, verifying Col. Sleeper's connection to the program. As Col. Sleeper was involved with Project Blue Book and both the Air Force and CIA have acknowledged this program and others like it were used to deceive the American public, any records generated by the FBI about Col. Sleeper would contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government as it relates to its research into UFOs and other relevant subject areas. The letter can also be found here: https://www.fold3.com/image/7282652/tucson-arizona-blank-page-8-us-project-blue-book-ufo-investigations-1947-1969

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. All documents will be provided to the general public without charge.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Records System, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for all known identities for Col. Sleeper in both main-file records and cross-reference records related to the Human Resources Research Institute, Institute for American Strategy, American Security Council Foundation, and Western Goals Foundation.

In the event that there are fees, I am willing to pay up to $100. Please advise if fees will exceed this amount so I might make an arrangement for requesting the records. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I look forward to your response within 20 working days, as the statute requires. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Sincerely,

Robert Skvarla, Jr.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Targeted Search: Gali District Disenfranchisement (2014)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all records, including emails (sent, received, CC’d, or BCC’d) and their attachments, created between April 1, 2014, and January 20, 2015, that discuss the disenfranchisement and "passportization" of ethnic Georgians in the Gali district of Abkhazia.

To ensure a comprehensive search of the National Security Council (NSC) and Department of State records, please include the following individuals and their associated email handles/aliases in your query:

1. Primary Department of State Targets:

Tom Malinowski (Assistant Secretary, DRL): malinowskit@state.gov, malinowskitp@state.gov, and any alias associated with "A/S Malinowski."
Richard Norland (Ambassador to Georgia): norlandr@state.gov, norlandrc@state.gov, and any alias associated with "Ambassador Norland" or "COM Tbilisi."
Eric Rubin (Deputy Assistant Secretary, EUR): rubine@state.gov.

2. National Security Council (NSC) Targets:

Celeste Wallander (Senior Director for Russia and Central Asia): cwallander@nsc.eop.gov.
Charles Kupchan (Senior Director for European Affairs): ckupchan@nsc.eop.gov.
Elizabeth Sherwood-Randall (White House Coordinator for Defense Policy and Arms Control): esherwood-randall@nsc.eop.gov.

NSC Records Management: Search for all records within the "Europe and Eurasia Directorate" and the "International Organizations and Alliance Directorate."

3. Specific Subject Matter Keywords & Metadata:
Please perform a keyword search against the "Subject" and "Body" fields of the above accounts for:

Gali AND Passport* (to include passportization/passports)
Ankvab AND Khajimba
Abkhaz* AND Disenfranchis*
Surkov AND Abkhaz*
Gali Georgians
IDP status
Аԥсны Аҳәынҭқарра 
Республика Абхазия

4. Search Constraints:
I request that the search include both the Open (Unclassified) Side and the Classified Side (SIPRNet/JWICS) records for the requested period. If any records are withheld under a (b)(1) national security exemption, I request a Vaughn Index describing the withheld material and the justification for the exemption.

Thank you to the Obama Presidential Library for the library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Robert Delaware

title: NSF FRR Grant 2237843

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: NSF FRR Grant 2237843
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Grant Unique Entity Identifier: GS3YEVSS12N6
Award Number (Abstract): 2237843
Principal Investigator: Hannah S Stuart

NSF Program(s):	FRR-Foundationl Rsrch Robotics
NSF Org: CMMI Division of Civil, Mechanical, and Manufacturing Innovation
Recipient:	REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, THE
Initial Amendment Date:	May 17, 2023
Start Date:	June 1, 2023
Program Manager:	Jordan Berg

Specifically, I request the following records, to the extent they are available and releasable:
1. The funded grant application in its final submitted form.
2. The abstract, specific aims, research strategy, budget pages, and other components included in the awarded application.
3. Any revised or resubmitted funded version of the application, if applicable.
4. All funded progress reports for the award, including each annual progress reports, any multi-year funded progress reports if applicable, and the final report or other closeout progress report, to the extent maintained by the foundation and releasable under FOIA.
5. Any other grant application materials maintained by the foundation that are part of the awarded application file and are subject to release under FOIA.

This request is limited to existing agency records. I am not requesting the creation of new records, explanations, analyses, or responses to questions.
If any portion of the requested material is exempt from disclosure, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. For each withheld portion, please identify the basis for withholding, including the applicable FOIA exemption(s).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Sterman

title: Grievances, 2026, Q1

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Grievances, 2026, Q1
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Provide a list or records regarding all grievance settlements including settlement amounts, union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable), date grievance settled, and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

2. Provide a list or records regarding all grievances in arbitration including union, date grievance filed, date arbitration started, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

3. Provide a total list of all grievances filed including union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2261817/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.
